settings.question: How long does it take to ferment?
settings.answer: 3–5 days at typical Indian room temperature (25–32°C). Cooler weather may extend it to 6–7 days. The bubbles in the airlock tell you when it's active.

settings.question: Is this really probiotic?
settings.answer: Yes. Wild lacto-fermentation produces 8+ strains of beneficial Lactobacillus bacteria.

settings.question: How long does it last?
settings.answer: Once ready, swap to the storage lid and refrigerate. Refrigerated ferments stay good for 2 weeks.

settings.question: Can I reuse the jar?
settings.answer: The jar lasts years — borosilicate glass, dishwasher-safe. The airlock is reusable indefinitely.

settings.heading: Spice Mix
settings.sub: Complete mix for authentic homemade ferment.
settings.ingredients:
Ground mustard powder (the wild starter)
Sea salt (controls fermentation)
Chilli powder (warmth + colour)
Ginger powder (digestive support)

settings.heading: Ferment Boost
settings.sub: Prebiotic blend that boosts good bacteria. Cuts ferment time to 3–5 days.
settings.ingredients:
Inulin (chicory root prebiotic fiber)
FOS (fructo-oligosaccharides)
Resistant dextrin (slow-fermenting fuel)
Feeds good bacteria, not bad ones
